text: The surgeon resects the affected segment of the colon, followed by resection of the mesocolon at both proximal and distal ends. The section area is marked under ICG fluorescence guidance.
The surgeon removes the anvil from the posterior aspect and introduces it through a colotomy into the healthy colon, positioning it near the knot area.
The surgeon uses a six-tip blue surform to section the affected segment of the colon at the proximal and distal ends. The resected segment is then placed into a bag for later extraction.

The surgeon extracts the anvil through the terminal part of the proximal colon, then applies a reinforced pull-string suture in the distal part. After completing the reinforcement, the proximal part of the anvil is extracted under visual control.
The surgeon performs a mechanical end-to-end anastomosis on the left colon, connecting the proximal and distal ends of the resected segment using a surgical stapler.
